program.name: BHP 2027 Graduate Program (Australia)
program.salary_text: BHP states it offers a competitive salary package, plus a performance-based bonus and an employee share program.
program.duration_text: The program is a 24-month BHP Graduate Program.
program.rotation_text: BHP describes roles as potentially being based in a residential community, a city office, or operating on a Fly-in Fly-out (FIFO) roster, depending on discipline and preferred location.

eligibility.work_rights: International students with a valid visa are welcome to apply. By February 2027, you must hold a visa with no work restrictions.
eligibility.graduation_window: You must have graduated within the past two years or be due to graduate by the end of 2026.
disciplines: Engineering (Mechanical, Mechatronics, Electrical, Civil, Geotechnical, Metallurgy / Minerals Processing, Chemical, Construction, Environmental, Instrumentation, Control & Automation)
Science (Hydrogeology, Environmental and Earth Sciences, Physics, Chemistry, Spatial Science (Surveying), Geographical Information Systems)
minimum_requirements: You must have graduated within the past two years or be due to graduate by the end of 2026.
International students must have a valid visa and, by February 2027, must hold a visa with no work restrictions.

stages.name: Stage 1: Application
stages.details: Apply online and add your resume.

stages.name: Stage 2: Online Assessment
stages.details: A video interview and cognitive testing.

stages.name: Stage 3: 1-on-1 Conversation with a Line Leader
Stage 6: Provide your Academic Transcript prior to your start date (Graduates only)

tips: Ask someone to review your resume for typos before submitting.
Read the application questions carefully and double check your responses, including your contact details.
Answer all questions when completing your candidate profile and the application form.
Cover letter and academic transcript are not required at the application stage (you can include a cover letter in the same file as your resume if you want).
For online assessments, read the email invitation carefully, use a quiet place, ensure your device is charged, and have a strong internet connection.
For the online video interview, treat it like a conversation and wear something comfortable.
Before the 1-on-1 conversation, review your resume, be ready to explain your motivation, and familiarise yourself with BHP’s values and purpose.

exec_themes: Run a portfolio of “tier one” mining assets and focus on commodities that are in demand both now and in the energy transition (notably copper and potash, alongside iron ore and higher-quality steelmaking coal).
Keep costs and operations tightly managed (operational excellence, productivity, cost discipline) to stay resilient in volatile markets.
Use a disciplined capital allocation framework to balance growth investment with shareholder returns (dividends).
Build a growth pipeline through major projects and phased expansions (e.g., potash at Jansen; copper growth at Escondida and Copper South Australia; advancing Vicuña).
Make sustainability central to strategy, with emphasis on climate targets and reducing operational emissions.
Strengthen “social value” outcomes (e.g., economic contributions, inclusion/diversity, Indigenous procurement, and community investment).
commercial_context.profit_engine: BHP’s profit engine is producing and selling large volumes of key commodities (especially iron ore and copper, and also steelmaking coal and emerging potash) from low-cost, long-life assets, while using tight cost control and disciplined capital allocation to generate strong cash flow and pay dividends.
commercial_context.headwinds: Exposure to a “volatile external environment” for commodity markets, plus cost pressures such as inflation (including higher labour costs) that can push unit costs up; and technology readiness and timing risk in delivering longer-term decarbonisation pathways (some decarbonisation technologies are not yet ready and development has slowed in parts).
commercial_context.esg: BHP frames sustainability as core to strategy and reports progress on: (1) climate—medium-term target to reduce operational (Scope 1 and 2) GHG emissions by at least 30% by FY2030 from an FY2020 baseline, and a long-term net zero goal for operational emissions by CY2050; (2) operational approach—renewable/low-emissions electricity procurement, diesel displacement trials (battery-electric haul trucks/locomotives and electrification of equipment), methane abatement, and planning to meet the FY2030 target through structural abatement rather than offsets; and (3) social factors—workforce safety/health focus, inclusion/diversity (including reported gender balance progress), and economic/social contributions (taxes/royalties, supplier spend, Indigenous procurement, community contributions).
commercial_context.recent_pivot: A recent strategic emphasis is accelerating and optimising growth options in copper and potash (including expected first production from Jansen potash by mid-2027, and expansion pathways in copper), alongside continued focus on capital discipline and maintaining shareholder returns.

notes: Program duration (24 months), close date (6 April 2026), and the February 2027 start date are stated on the BHP career portal job ad.
Broader Australia program page confirms relevant degree areas and mentions FIFO/relocation expectations in Australia, and lists Australian states (WA, SA, QLD) on the same page.

notes: The stages (Apply online; Online Assessment; 1-on-1 conversation; Offer; Medical for site roles; Provide transcript prior to start date) and practical application tips are stated on the Australia graduate and student programs page.
The job ad also notes that recruitment checks may include police/criminal background checks, medical, drug and alcohol testing, due diligence checks, right to work checks, and/or reference checks.

notes: This summary is based on BHP’s official investor reporting pages and climate plan pages. Some fields (especially ‘headwinds’ and ‘recent_pivot’) require interpretation because BHP discusses them across multiple sections and often in broad terms rather than as a single labeled list.
‘Profit engine’ is phrased in plain English by combining BHP’s stated portfolio/strategy and financial performance descriptions; it is not a single quote from one section.

ESG items include explicit targets and described actions; broader ESG topics (e.g., tailings, human rights) are referenced on BHP’s site but not exhaustively summarized here because the request is for concise, high-signal context.
